hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The feature enables the comparison engine to treat a continuous vertical sequence of single-pixel differences as noise rather than an error, effectively ignoring one potential alignment issue in the image stream.
The literal hex string 89504e470d0a1a0a which represents the first 12 bytes of a standard PNG file header, ensuring the returned buffer begins with this exact glyph sequence.
The output must terminate every key=value pair with a standard Unix newline character (ASCII 10), as shown by the embedded '\n' at the end of the sample values.

context_pointers.vague_reference: reference the diff_size parsing logic from the graphics_auditor.py decorators
context_pointers.actual_meaning: This constraint refers to the specific JSON key layout (key: 'diff_size', value: 'WIDTHxHEIGHT') that the system expects for the diff dimensions, detailed in the utility parser.

context_pointers.vague_reference: consult the legacy diff_engine module for the exact key used
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The constraint requires a specific key named 'color_tolerance' within a 'diff_options' object; while similar to C005, the exact casing and nesting depth may vary in older implementation branches.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_image_comparison.json.description: Compares two PNG images supplied either as file-backed images or in-memory image bytes and reports whether the rendered pixels look the same under the requested comparison options.

feature2_comparison_option_conflict.json.description: Rejects a comparison request that asks for strict pixel matching and perceptual tolerance at the same time, because those modes are mutually exclusive.

feature3_diff_image_generation.json.description: Builds a PNG diff image that preserves matching pixels and paints differing or out-of-bounds pixels with the requested highlight color, returning either a written image or an in-memory PNG.

feature4_diff_option_conflict.json.description: Rejects a diff-generation request that asks for strict pixel matching and perceptual tolerance at the same time, because those modes are mutually exclusive.

_constraints.input_condition: Input JSON is missing required fields

_constraints.output_rule: Read images from file paths specified in input
Read images from paths if provided, otherwise from JSON map values
Apply perceptual tolerance to pixel comparison
Compare pixels with exact RGB match (no tolerance)
Ignore one continuous vertical column of pixels where differences exist
Return error if mutually exclusive options are present
Generate diff image with highlighted regions
Compare pixels with exact RGB match
Return error if mutually exclusive options are present
Output lines are separated by newlines
exact_values:

candidate=PATH
input_transport=file
reference_size=50x50
candidate_size=50x50
comparison=look_same
